Package io.mdudel.zenoh.purejava.transport

Class TlsConfig

java.lang.Object
io.mdudel.zenoh.purejava.transport.TlsConfig
public final class TlsConfig extends Object
Immutable TLS configuration for TlsTransport. Build one with the fluent TlsConfig.Builder and reuse it across many transport instances — the underlying SSLContext is materialised once at build time.

Minimal-viable config is a trust store path + password; the server-cert path is common in accreditation environments where an internal CA is not in the JVM's default cacerts. Optional client keystore + password enables mTLS.

Everything else has JDK-safe defaults: TLSv1.3 + TLSv1.2 enabled, hostname verification on, JVM default cipher suites, 15 s handshake timeout.

All KeyStore / TrustManager / KeyManager wiring happens in TlsConfig.Builder.build() and any GeneralSecurityException or IOException is wrapped in IllegalStateException so callers can treat the builder outcome as either "usable config" or "startup-time misconfiguration". Runtime handshake failures remain regular TransportExceptions in the transport.

  • Field Details

    • DEFAULT_PROTOCOLS

      public static final List<String> DEFAULT_PROTOCOLS
      Enabled protocols in preference order. TLSv1.3 first, TLSv1.2 fallback.

    • DEFAULT_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_MS

  • Method Details

    • sslContext

      public SSLContext sslContext()
      The underlying SSLContext. Exposed for consumers (e.g. WsTransport) that need to configure an SSLEngine rather than an SSLSocket.
